evals.question_short: Custom GUC sizing shared memory — registration sequence

assertions.text: Picks DefineCustomIntVariable as the right typed entry point
Selects PGC_POSTMASTER and justifies it via the shmem-allocation-is-fixed-at-startup invariant
Sets GUC_UNIT_KB so SQL accepts size suffixes like '128MB'
Requires MarkGUCPrefixReserved (not just EmitWarningsOnPlaceholders) and explains it must be called AFTER all DefineCustom* calls
Mentions placeholder removal — placeholders created when conf mentions the GUC before extension load
Cites a specific source path (guc.h, guc.c, or worker_spi.c) with file:line for at least one claim
Notes that without MarkGUCPrefixReserved, parallel-worker startup can later trip over orphan placeholders OR mentions the typo-detection benefit explicitly

evals.question_short: One-shot dynamic bgworker, never restart, join from launcher

assertions.text: Uses RegisterDynamicBackgroundWorker (not the static RegisterBackgroundWorker) and explains why (latter only callable from _PG_init with process_shared_preload_libraries_in_progress)
Sets bgw_restart_time = BGW_NEVER_RESTART
Mentions that proc_exit(0) (return 0) also disables restart regardless of bgw_restart_time
Sets bgw_notify_pid = MyProcPid and explains it enables SIGUSR1 on state transitions so wait calls work efficiently
Uses WaitForBackgroundWorkerShutdown(handle) for joining
Shows BGWORKER_SHMEM_ACCESS + BGWORKER_BACKEND_DATABASE_CONNECTION flags for a DB-touching worker
Specifies worker main signature as pg_noreturn PGDLLEXPORT void f(Datum) and that signal handlers go BEFORE BackgroundWorkerUnblockSignals
Cites a specific source path (bgworker.h or worker_spi.c) with file:line

evals.question_short: Extension-driven parallel via ParallelContext + DSM + workers vs leader GUCs

assertions.text: Lists the full lifecycle: EnterParallelMode, CreateParallelContext, estimate, InitializeParallelDSM, allocate+insert, LaunchParallelWorkers, WaitForParallelWorkersToFinish, DestroyParallelContext, ExitParallelMode
Specifies worker signature `void f(dsm_segment*, shm_toc*)` and PGDLLEXPORT requirement
States the reserved TOC key range 0xFFFFFFFFFFFF0001..0xFFFFFFFFFFFF000F and recommends small ints for extension keys
Lists forbidden worker actions: no DB writes, no temp tables, no sequences, no non-buffer locks unilaterally, no PARALLEL UNSAFE functions
Confirms workers see leader's GUCs at launch via serialization through PARALLEL_KEY_GUC / ParallelWorkerMain and that later SETs don't propagate
Warns that pcxt->nworkers_launched may be less than requested and must be checked
Mentions estimate-then-allocate symmetry as a gotcha (shm_toc_estimate_chunk + estimate_keys must precede InitializeParallelDSM)
Cites a specific source path (parallel.h, parallel.c) with file:line
